novel_features.name: Flag Blast Radius
novel_features.description: Find every insight, dashboard, experiment, and survey that references a flag before you archive or rename it.
novel_features.rationale: Requires scanning insights, dashboards, experiments, and surveys for flag-key references in one pass — no cross-resource search exists in the UI or API.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before archiving or renaming a flag to prevent breaking dashboards and experiments silently.

novel_features.name: Flag Rollout Health
novel_features.description: Go/no-go confidence for a flag ramp — error rate and key metric movement correlated with flag exposure.
novel_features.rationale: Requires time-windowed join of three event streams (flag_called, errors, success metric) that only the local store can correlate sub-second.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before ramping a flag to 100% to catch regressions that aren't visible in overall metrics.

novel_features.name: LLM Cost Attribution
novel_features.description: Break down LLM spend by feature flag variant — see whether the expensive model variant pays for itself.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining LLM trace costs against feature_flag_called events on distinct_id + session window. Neither the LLM nor flags UI does this join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when evaluating whether to promote a more expensive LLM variant based on actual cost-per-conversion.

novel_features.name: Persons At-Risk
novel_features.description: Surface which users in a cohort are going quiet and recently hit errors — before they churn.
novel_features.rationale: Requires per-person event-frequency baseline from the events store correlated with error records by distinct_id across a cohort.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in weekly retention reviews to prioritize proactive outreach before churn events.

novel_features.name: Event Property Drift
novel_features.description: Catch tracking regressions — properties that silently disappeared from an event between two time windows.
novel_features.rationale: Requires bucketing event records from two distinct time slices and diffing property key sets. The UI shows current state only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use after a deploy to catch silent schema changes that corrupt ongoing experiments and dashboards.

novel_features.name: Experiment Pre-Check
novel_features.description: Know today whether an experiment will reach significance this sprint, or needs traffic adjustment now.
novel_features.rationale: Combines live exposure counts with required sample size and a power projection formula — PostHog shows current count only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use at the start of each sprint to surface experiments that need traffic changes before they run out of time.

novel_features.name: Flag Stale Finder
novel_features.description: List flags that haven't been evaluated in N days — cleanup candidates before they accumulate.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-references flag last_updated timestamps with event definitions to find flags with zero recent evaluation coverage.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in quarterly flag cleanup sprints to identify dead code paths safely.

novel_features.name: Dashboard Health
novel_features.description: Find broken dashboards before a stakeholder meeting does — stale data, deleted cohorts, archived flags.
novel_features.rationale: Joins dashboard filter definitions against live cohort/flag/event state in the local store. No cross-dashboard audit surface exists in PostHog.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before weekly business reviews to catch broken insight tiles that would embarrass the data team.

narrative.headline: Every PostHog resource in one CLI — with offline search, agent-native output, and cross-resource analytics no dashboard can do.
narrative.value_prop: posthog-pp-cli syncs your flags, insights, experiments, persons, errors, and LLM traces to a local SQLite store. Query anything offline, run compound analytics across resources the UI keeps separate, and pipe results directly to agents or scripts.
narrative.auth_narrative: Uses your PostHog personal API key (phx_...). Set POSTHOG_API_KEY or run `posthog-pp-cli auth set-token`. Supports both US (app.posthog.com) and EU (eu.posthog.com) instances via POSTHOG_HOST.

quickstart.comment: Connect to your PostHog instance with your personal API key
Sync flags, insights, experiments, persons, and errors to local store
List all feature flags with rollout rules
Full-text search across synced flags, insights, experiments, and persons — offline
Find everything that references a flag before archiving

troubleshoots.fix: Run `posthog-pp-cli auth set-token phx_YOUR_KEY` with your personal API key (not the project token)
Check your project ID: `posthog-pp-cli projects list` — then set POSTHOG_PROJECT_ID
PostHog limits analytics queries to 240/min. Add `--rate-limit 4` to cap request rate, or reduce query frequency

narrative.when_to_use: Use posthog-pp-cli when an agent task requires querying, diffing, or combining PostHog data across more than one resource type — flag state vs error events, LLM costs vs variant performance, experiment exposure vs significance. It is faster than the web UI for bulk reads and produces structured JSON that agents can consume directly without parsing HTML.

recipes.title: Find flags safe to archive
recipes.explanation: List flag keys with no evaluation events in 60 days — safe cleanup candidates

recipes.title: Pre-ramp safety check
recipes.explanation: Check error-rate delta for flag-exposed users in the last 48h before ramping a flag to 100%

recipes.title: Weekly experiment briefing
recipes.explanation: Surface experiments that will not reach significance this sprint unless you add traffic

recipes.title: LLM cost by flag variant
recipes.explanation: Compare LLM spend across A/B variants to decide if GPT-4o outperforms GPT-4o-mini in your context

recipes.title: Dashboard audit before all-hands
recipes.command: posthog-pp-cli dashboard health --stale-days 7 --json | jq '.[] | select(.issues | length > 0)'
recipes.explanation: Surface dashboards with broken filters or stale data before a stakeholder presentation

trigger_phrases: what errors are spiking in PostHog
is it safe to ramp this flag
